title: Plot a Radar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_radar
text: data Data frame containing data plot. axis_var String. Column name variable supplies axes (dimensions) radar. target String. Column name variable supplies values plot. labels String. Column name variable containing axis labels display around radar. colors String. Column name variable supplies color grouping. plot shows one polygon per group. maincat String. Column used choose axis labels. NULL, labels taken first color group. cvec Named vector colors, one per group. Default NULL (WJP contrast pair #482d8b / #f26b21 applied). order String. title: Plot a Radar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_radar
text: ggplot object representing radar plot.

title: Plot a Radar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_radar
code: library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  # Always load the WJP fonts wjp_fonts()  # Opinions about authorities, by gender data4radar <- WJPr::gpp %>%   select(gend, starts_with("q49")) %>%   mutate(     gend   = as.double(unclass(gend)),     across(starts_with("q49"), \(x) as.double(unclass(x))),     gender = case_when(gend == 1 ~ "Male", gend == 2 ~ "Female"),     across(starts_with("q49"), \(x) case_when(x <= 2 ~ 1, x <= 99 ~ 0))   ) %>%   group_by(gender) %>%   summarise(across(starts_with("q49"), \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)) %>%   pivot_longer(!gender, names_to = "category", values_to = "percentage") %>%   mutate(axis_label = category)  wjp_radar(   data4radar,   axis_var = "category",   target   = "percentage",   labels   = "axis_label",   colors   = "gender",   cvec     = c("Male" = "#482d8b", "Female" = "#f26b21") )

title: Plot a Rose Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_rose
code: library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  # Always load the WJP fonts wjp_fonts()  # Opinions about authorities as a single-unit profile data4rose <- WJPr::gpp %>%   select(starts_with("q49")) %>%   mutate(     across(starts_with("q49"), \(x) as.double(unclass(x))),     across(starts_with("q49"), \(x) case_when(x <= 2 ~ 1, x <= 99 ~ 0))   ) %>%   summarise(across(starts_with("q49"), \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)) %>%   pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "category", values_to = "percentage") %>%   mutate(axis_label = category)  wjp_rose(   data4rose,   target   = "percentage",   grouping = "category",   labels   = "axis_label" )

what: Plot a Slope Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_slope
title: Plot a Slope Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_slope
text: wjp_slope() takes data frame long-format data returns ggplot object slope chart following WJP style guidelines. Slope charts compare values exactly two points time. line defined colors variable. Values expected 0-100 percentage scale.

title: Plot a Slope Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_slope
code: wjp_slope(   data,   target,   grouping,   colors = NULL,   cvec = NULL,   labels = NULL,   repel = FALSE,   ngroups = NULL,   ptheme = WJP_theme() )

title: Plot a Slope Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_slope
text: data Data frame containing data plot. target String. Column name variable supplies values plot. grouping String. Column name numeric variable supplies two X-axis values (usually years). colors String. Column name variable defines lines color grouping. Default NULL (single line drawn). cvec Named vector colors, one per line. Names match values colors variable. Default NULL (WJP palette, see wjp_palette(), applied). labels String. Column name variable containing value labels display. Default NULL (labels). repel Logical. TRUE, ggrepel package used avoid overlapping labels. Default FALSE. ngroups Grouping vector lines. Retained backwards compatibility; lines now grouped colors automatically. ptheme ggplot theme apply. Default WJP_theme().

title: Plot a Slope Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_slope
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot a Slope Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_slope
code: library(dplyr)  # Always load the WJP fonts wjp_fonts()  # Percentage of people that trust their institutions, by gender data4slopes <- WJPr::gpp %>%   filter(year %in% c(2017, 2019)) %>%   mutate(     q1a    = as.double(unclass(q1a)),     gend   = as.double(unclass(gend)),     trust  = case_when(q1a <= 2 ~ 1, q1a <= 4 ~ 0),     gender = case_when(gend == 1 ~ "Male", gend == 2 ~ "Female")   ) %>%   group_by(year, gender) %>%   summarise(trust = mean(trust, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, .groups = "drop") %>%   mutate(value_label = paste0(round(trust, 0), "%"))  wjp_slope(   data4slopes,   target   = "trust",   grouping = "year",   colors   = "gender",   labels   = "value_label",   cvec     = c("Male" = "#482d8b", "Female" = "#f26b21"),   repel    = TRUE )

title: WJPr 1.1.0
text: New wjp_palette() function exposing official WJP categorical color palette. chart functions now fall back palette cvec supplied, charts stay -brand default (previously fell back default ggplot2 hues). wjp_lines() wjp_slope() longer require ngroups parameter: lines grouped colors variable automatically. functions also work without colors (single series drawn). ngroups retained backwards compatibility. wjp_dumbbells() gains alternating strip background (visual consistency wjp_dots()), automatic label positions labpos supplied, support named cvec vectors matched cgroups. wjp_lollipops() gains labels, order, ptheme parameters. Value labels generated automatically labels supplied. wjp_edgebars() labels parameter now optional defaults grouping values. wjp_dots() automatically enables per-group opacities shapes opacities shapes supplied. wjp_divbars() enables custom ordering automatically order supplied (custom_order flag retained backwards compatibility).

title: WJPr 1.1.0
text: wjp_dumbbells(): color renamed colors (old name still works). wjp_radar() wjp_rose(): order_var renamed order (old name still works).

title: WJPr 1.1.0
text: Fixed bug wjp_gauge() invisible padding segment received visible palette color cvec supplied, drawing full circle instead semicircle. Fixed bug wjp_dumbbells() order parameter ignored. diffmeans() now returns results explicitly (previously value returned invisibly). wjp_lines() value labels longer get clipped values close 100%: label flips point instead.

title: WJPr 1.1.0
text: Value labels now share typography across charts (Lato bold, 10 pt, ink #4a4a49). Grid lines harmonized single gray (#d1cfd1) across charts. Category axis text harmonized (#524F4C, left-aligned) across horizontal charts. Horizontal charts (wjp_dots(), wjp_dumbbells(), wjp_lollipops(), wjp_edgebars()) now consistently display first row data top chart. wjp_check_data() gains support type = "groupbars". wjp_groupbars(): confidence intervals drawn, value labels now placed end full bar (gray complement), aligned single column, instead next upper interval whisker. wjp_groupbars(): strip_position = "top" single-column layouts now renders group titles horizontal headers group instead rotated (clipped) strips right.

title: WJPr 1.0.1
text: Updated wjp_groupbars() documentation gallery images show corrected grouped-bar layout neutral complement bars, confidence intervals, national bar, optional percentage axis.

title: WJPr 1.0.0
text: Fixed bugs preventing wjp_radar() plot specific data structures. Fixed bugs preventing wjp_dots() plot specific data structures. Change way wjp_dots() calculated added Confidence Intervals charts. wjp_slope() added. diff_means() added.
